steps.title: Use vertex form
steps.content:
The function is written as a squared expression plus a constant:
$$f(x)=(x+7)^2+4$$
A squared term is smallest when the expression inside the parentheses equals $0$.

steps.title: Find where the square is smallest
steps.content:
Set the inside of the square equal to $0$:
$$x+7=0$$
At that input, the function reaches its minimum.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content:
Graph
$$f(x)=(x+7)^{2}+4$$
and read the $x$-coordinate of the vertex. The answer is -7.
